_meta.description: Supplementary reference data for UI designers. Contains all registries, enums, config schemas, venue details, and operational modes — everything needed to populate dropdowns, filters, and understand domain bounds. Use alongside the OpenAPI spec.

ui_data.controls: Client-side mock data vs API calls

presets.ci: CI smoke tests, deterministic, no cache persistence
presets.mock: Local dev/UAT (default), interactive state, persists to .local-dev-cache/
presets.api-real: Test APIs against real cloud data

strategy.$note: Strategy UI fetches /api/strategies/* from execution-results-api. Backtest runs use a separate local Python process (port 5001), not an API service.

settlement.$note: Settlement routes live in trading-analytics-api (/settlement/* and /settlements/*). No separate settlement-api needed.

live-health-monitor.$note: Live health monitor calls /api/positions, /api/risk, /api/health from execution-results-api. Manual trade instructions route to execution-service directly (VITE_EXECUTION_API_URL).

logs-dashboard.$note: Logs dashboard calls /api/v1/audit/trail and /audit/trail/services from batch-audit-api. Manifest confirms batch-audit-api serves_ui includes logs-dashboard-ui.

odum-website.$note: Self-contained Next.js app with built-in API routes (Firebase Auth + Firestore). No separate API service. Uses port 3000 (Next.js default) instead of 5184 to match Next.js convention.

user-management.$note: Admin UI for user lifecycle management (onboard/modify/offboard). OAuth admin auth. Mock mode uses client-side fetch intercepts. Backend API TBD — currently calls external APIs (GitHub, Slack, MS Graph, GCP) via mock layer.
